root_motivation: Make MDX a first-class output path for the MQO fleet so that cellset/drill-through workloads and Excel/pivot clients can be served without burdening every downstream prompt with MDX structural rules (R6/R7/R10/R11/R13). The compiler encodes these rules as compile-time invariants instead of leaving them to prompt discipline.
user_persona: AtScale platform engineer wiring the backend router (PRD-mqo-backend-router) to the cellset path; also integration tests and CI pipelines that verify the MDX output shape before it reaches an OLAP engine.

unfakeable_metric.name: acceptance_tests_passing
unfakeable_metric.harness_command: cargo test --release --workspace 2>&1 | grep -E '^test result' | awk '{print $4}' | paste -sd+ | bc

acceptance_criteria.description: ≥6 golden BoundMqo → MDX pairs compile exactly (normalized string-equal). Covers: measure-only, measure+dim, multi-dim CROSSJOIN, three-part cube name, Member filter in WHERE, two measures on COLUMNS, full query with WITH+WHERE.
acceptance_criteria.test: tests::ac1_minimal_measure_only, tests::ac1_measure_with_one_dimension, tests::ac1_measure_with_two_dimensions_crossjoin, tests::ac1_three_part_cube_name, tests::ac1_member_filter_in_where, tests::ac1_two_measures_on_columns, tests::ac1_full_query_golden

acceptance_criteria.description: Every emitted MDX includes NON EMPTY on the row axis and a fully-qualified cube name (R10/R13).
A calculated measure pulls its MDX-dependency hierarchies onto the axes (R6); dep-hierarchies covered by bound dimensions are not duplicated.
A calc-group member literal is emitted verbatim from bound metadata into a WITH MEMBER clause (R7); CalcGroupMember filter appears in WHERE slicer.
A semi-additive measure without a trigger level produces MdxCompileError::SemiAdditiveMissingTrigger (exit non-zero); error message names the measure (R11).
cargo test --release passes; cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings passes (clippy clean).
acceptance_criteria.test: cargo test --release --workspace exits 0; cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings exits 0

acceptance_criteria.description: Integration CLI test: mqo-mdx binary invoked via std::process::Command compiles a golden BoundMqo JSON file and asserts on stdout/exit-code.
Range filters in MQO.filters are handled gracefully (not silently dropped without documentation).
acceptance_criteria.test: test::range_filter_handled_in_codegen or doc comment on build_where_clause

scope: Accept BoundMqo JSON (from --bound flag or stdin) and emit MDX SELECT to stdout.
Enforce R6, R7, R10, R11, R13 as compile-time invariants.
Depends on mqo-spec git dep and serde_json, clap, thiserror.
non_goals: MDX parsing or round-trip: this is a codegen-only path.
SQL codegen: that is the responsibility of the SQL backend crate.
Execution or sending MDX to an OLAP engine.
Optimization of the MDX (e.g. subselects, named sets for degenerate single-member axes).
Support for MDX functions beyond what the bound metadata supplies.
Range-filter-to-MDX translation (deliberately omitted with documented rationale).

five_whys_trace.q: Why does the MQO fleet need an MDX backend at all?
five_whys_trace.a: Some request shapes — asymmetric axes, ragged hierarchies, drill-through, pivot cellsets — are natively expressed in MDX and cannot be served by flat SQL without lossy translation.

five_whys_trace.q: Why compile at the MQO layer rather than letting the router emit ad-hoc MDX?
five_whys_trace.a: R6/R7/R10/R11/R13 are structural rules that, if scattered across prompt text, will be violated in proportion to context-window pressure. Making them compile-time invariants enforces correctness regardless of the LLM.

five_whys_trace.q: Why does this ship as a separate crate rather than a module inside mqo-backend-router?
five_whys_trace.a: The router decides WHICH backend; this crate decides HOW to emit MDX. Separation means the MDX compiler can be tested, fuzz-targeted, and replaced independently — and keeps the router's dependency graph narrow.

ambiguities_resolved.question: Should Range filters in MQO.filters be converted to MDX slicer members?
ambiguities_resolved.resolution: No. Range filters don't map cleanly to MDX slicer members; they're documented as intentionally omitted with a comment in build_where_clause. NON EMPTY on the row axis serves as the structural guard. This is a non-goal.

ambiguities_resolved.question: Should the binary accept stdin as well as --bound <file>?
ambiguities_resolved.resolution: v0.1 only supports --bound <file>. Stdin is a MAY for a follow-on PR.
